page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a modest compute profile. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng tests fall into the poor and weak categories, respectively. However, memory bandwidth for read-only operations remains average, reaching 398.82 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 40.7 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Large block write operations are weaker at 23.27 GB/sec. Geekbench testing shows average single-core capabilities but weak multi-core scaling, which is also reflected in weak software compilation and image processing scores.

Given its shared CPU allocation and resource density, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. It is well-suited for light database operations, basic text processing, and small-scale language model inference, where it achieves average prompt processing speeds of 402.78 tokens per second on a 135M model. It is less optimal for high-throughput web serving or intensive multi-threaded compilation tasks.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 4 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage, dedicated GPUs, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark results show average single-core performance and memory read bandwidth, contrasted by weaker multi-core scaling and write speeds. Due to its shared CPU allocation, this server represents a budget-friendly option for workloads with intermittent resource demands. It is best suited for development environments, light database operations, basic text processing, and low-intensity tasks like small-scale LLM inference using models under 135M parameters.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server with shared AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory for light development and basic database workloads.
